The marketers that aren't getting enough traffic, 99 out of 100 times, it's because they don't know how to turn that traffic into sales and new customers. See the entire game in terms of generating a consistent and reliable income for yourself, the whole game of being able to scale your business, grow your business bigger and bigger and bigger over time starts with the foundation of you being able to turn a dollar of traffic into a dollar, $2, $3 or more in the form of new sales and new customers. You need to start having conversions in place and you can do that. Two things you can do that with. A, you can use Facebook's built in conversion tracking, which is not perfect. But guess what? Nothing's really going to be perfect at the end of the day. You need to start measuring your opt-ins. You need to start measuring your sales. What you want to do is on your squeeze page, whatever page they opt in after that, put your thank you code from Facebook. You can go to Facebook and say, I want to create a conversion code. Put that on the thank you page and then put a tracking code on your purchase thank you page. You've got two pieces of code. One goes on your thank you page after they opt in. The next one goes on your thank you page after they buy. If you just do that conversion tracking and that's done in WordPress, it's done in HTML, they're plugins to do it a few years in OptimizePress. It will pretty much have little boxes you can do that. That's your first thing. Before you ever get anything else because A, it's a simple thing, but most people forget about it until they're running traffic and then their stats are skewed because they've had three days of traffic, no conversion tracking in place. Once you've got your conversion tracking in place, create a fan page. What you want to do in Facebook is go ahead and create a fan page and when you're buying traffic in Facebook, Facebook's smart. They say, okay Bob, do you want to send your traffic outside of Facebook? We're going to charge you more money from it. Or if you send your Facebook traffic to a page inside of Facebook, it does nothing but inflate the views and the traffic stats of Facebook, which pretty much helps them get more money. So you want to do is basically create a fan page and this is very important. If you're going to do any kind of Facebook marketing, you want to create a fan page and you're trying to generate likes for it. What happens, you can advertise to people who like your page for about 10 cents where if you're marketing to a non-fan, you're going to pay about 30 cents. So just as a byproduct, you will generate fans. So what happens inside of your fan page, you can use, not to get too technical, but we've all seen these little tabs here. That allows you to put your pop-up or your landing page on top of your competitors. And regardless of what niche you're in, that's extremely powerful. You're talking to, I mean, you can very easily get 10, 15,000 clicks in an hour, depending on your niche, depending on your product, and depending on how aggressive you want to go. But the key is focus on which traffic is going to give you the biggest bang for your buck. Because I mean, there's no shortage of traffic between Facebook, Google. You've got the Google Display Network. You've got Site Scout. You've got MSN. You've got solo emails. And that's only scratching the surface. I mean, there are tons of places to get traffic from. The key is make sure that your product, whatever you're promoting, you need to be, and this is where most people really screw up. They're not really willing to split test. It sounds, you know, split testing is kind of like high school. Everybody's talking about what they're doing, but nobody's really doing it. It's down. It's like, well, you know, I'm a split testing ninja. And you go to the web page and there's like not a single piece of tracking code on it in any way, shape, or form. You really need to absolutely get used to split testing. Now, I want to tell you, I don't care how much traffic you get. If you're getting the wrong quality traffic, or if the traffic's not targeted, doesn't do you any good. I mean, let's face it, not too many men are buying makeup. I don't care how many S.T. Lotter banners I see on my websites, where I'm, you know, I'm just not going, gee whiz, I really need some of this S.T. Lotter stuff to have smaller pores, bigger eyes, and brighter skin. You know, wouldn't really be effective to me. But if you're a wall and you're like, okay, great. One of the things you really want to look at is when you're looking at getting traffic, you have to understand traffic comes in a few different variations. You have niche traffic, which would be like internet marketing. That's one of probably the more popular niches. Most people don't realize that's actually a very small sliver of traffic. It's not like the big be all to end all. If you get into larger traffic, that would be like a dating niche, or if you're into the dating, or if you want to go into like mass market. And I'm going to stress to you, if you want to really make, if you want to be able to buy a lot of traffic, look at mass market type offers. That's weight loss, skin care, health, fitness, wellness, investment. I know it could be argued that internet marketing is kind of like a make money investment business opportunity. Yeah, I guess maybe how you want to argue potentially it is. But if you want to go into the mass market offers, and a good way to do that, I'm just telling you this is the ultimate research tool, by the way. Turn on your TV and look at what's on TV. We complain about pay per click. It's a dollar per click. My gosh. I mean, what kind of place is this? Look at it from the standpoint of TV. When you look at, well, I give an example, there's a very popular product on now called Dancers, but I know where it's a family friendly show here, but Dancers, but watch that commercial for like an hour, 30 minutes, and you realize they are talking about how ladies can get fit, how they can develop a better backside, so to speak. The reality of that is those commercials are extremely, I mean, they're very, very, very expensive. And because of that, you need to look at it. If you're going mass market, and a good example, just that's been in the news yesterday, Kevin Trudeau just went to jail for actually kind of been in jail and out of jail, but after he originally got his, I guess, FTC fine or whatever you want to call it, he aired his commercial. Think about this 32,000 more times. Let that sink in. 32,000 more airings of a 30 minute show. The reality of, if you've got something converting, and I really stress to you, make sure your stuff's converting because the more you split test it, you can start going out to larger and larger audiences. And I'm going to give you a good example. We became one of the top 100 affiliates at Clickbank. Dumbest thing I've ever done in the world, and I'm going to get to that because I probably could have had much more money I could have made out of the back end of that, but long story short, whenever we decided, okay, I'm going to have no clue that even anything existed, to make that money, we stepped out of the internet marketing niche. Most people think, well, Charles, you made it all in the internet marketing niche. No, if you're in the internet marketing niche, on a good day, you probably make about 100, 200 sales a day of a good internet marketing product every day with paid traffic, 200 sales. If you're rocking 200 sales, you're really hustling. The minute you step out into fitness and weight loss, your sales go to thousands per day. And the key is, I guarantee, and I am telling you because I need to lose weight myself. If you walk into any mall in America and you ask 100 people, would you like to build an email list, they're going to tell you it sells like a scam, or would you like to lose weight, get rich, get thinner, grow more hair, and have a nicer car, nine times out of 10, they will say, where's that? Sign me up. You have to look at getting mass scale type offers. The fitness, the skin care, the weight loss, because the minute you do a mass scale offer, you can buy traffic from virtually anywhere because we're dealing with supply and demand. Internet marketing, your supply is about such, your demand is about such, which jacks up the price. When you go mass market, we're talking fitness, weight loss, retirement, those things, it's easier to scale those particular niches up, literally to the hundreds of millions of dollars a year. And a good example is Beachbody. They focus on health, fitness, exercise. I mean, they're the guys behind the PX90. But the reality of it is, millions and millions and millions of Americans have bought that particular workout and do it. The offer didn't work, right, like it didn't convert well, it wasn't successful, but they continued to test and test and test and got to the point where they found the offer, they found the hook, they found what converted. That's why what's incredibly important for you to understand across the board, I don't care who it is, this is Charles, myself included, does not matter who it is, you've got to understand that not every funnel that you create, not every offer that you create is going to be a grand slam home run, right, you're going to create some funnels that are going to do extremely well, you'll create, those of you that are using the six figure funnel formula, you'll create funnels that are well into the six figures. We just had a client that had his first six figure month not that long ago, and then you'll have some that don't do six figures, maybe some that do 50 grand, and you will have some funnels that you roll out, some offers that will be total duds. promotions that Agora had ever released, it came out of a division of Agora called Stansbury Research, and it generated like, I don't even know, it could be over 20 million dollars worth of new front end customers that were each paying $49 a year, so you do the math, 20 million dollars, close to 20 million dollars at $49 per sale, shows you how many new customers they acquired, that doesn't even include the back end at all, anyway I said to Mark, I said, so when do you guys begin working on the next offer, like the next front end, the next funnel if you will, and he looked at me and he said, we never stop, we never stop working on the next funnel, he said because Todd, you've got to understand that eight out of ten offers that we roll out won't work, they either won't be at break even or, you know, they just won't work, and now there's a huge lesson right there for you because that's Agora with one of the best teams of copywriters as it relates to alternative health and financial in the world, and so if Agora is putting out two out of every ten offers or winners, like winners, not winners to the level of the end of America, but they're winners, you can't expect that every single offer is going to crush it right out of the gate, and so what Charles was saying is so dead on in that that's why you test, that's why I included that module in the Six Figure Funnel Formula Training Program on testing because it's testing that allows you to take any funnel no matter where it's at right now and methodically and scientifically improve it over time, not with guesswork, not with assumptions, not with guesses, but with metrics, with data, very easy to do, unfortunately lots of marketers like Charles said they don't do it or if they do it they do it incorrectly, but enough of me rambling. So Charles, there's a bunch of questions that came in, so let me see here, I want to go back to, so somebody said Dave asked, Dave said, I'm hearing pay a lot, is free traffic a waste of time? It's one of those things, I can tell you this, I got my start in 1999 using free traffic, first month made no money, second month would pay traffic, did about $10,000, guess what, times have changed, the issue that you're dealing with free traffic is you don't know the quality of that, you don't, somebody could be searching for greenhouse plans and somehow your site for cures to green fever happened to like line up and you're getting something like, man this green, I came here for greenhouse plans and this guy's website's about the green fever, you know, you can issue is you can't take the traffic, you can't measure it very actually because you don't know the quality of that traffic, is it buyer traffic, is it people looking for free stuff, is it people looking for free downloads, is it looking for free information and you can't qualify that, so basically in my opinion free traffic, SEO is like huge toilet paper, it's valuable to somebody, just not me and the biggest thing is I cannot scale a business on SEO, you know, my thing is if I have to rely on the Google guards to come down and say you know what Charles you've been good this week, let's throw you some traffic, hallelujah, guess what, that happened that way and if your entire business is built on Google, now I don't know about you but they seem to have this zoo, they've got the panda, the penguins, the lions, tigers and bears and every month they, you know, it's like release the Kraken, let's change up the search engine rankings, SEO is always a game as the black hat guys figured out Google is making changes and unfortunately because of that all the legitimate people typically are caught up in that big mess, so don't worry about anything SEO, you need to worry about paid traffic where you can measure it, you can qualify it and you can scale it. Yeah and I think that's a great piece of advice for everybody, you know, what Charles was saying, the big thing is so that you understand what he means or what I mean, what we mean when we say scale, there's a couple of things, right? So number one, let's say that you get the number one ranking on Google for the number one keyword for your marketplace, so you're thrilled, right, to marketers that are focused on search engine optimization that are focused, you know, solely on article distribution, press release, you know, news release distribution, video distribution, that's what they're solely focused on, that's like the holy grail, right, getting that number one ranking for the number one keyword. Well what happens when you get that number one ranking for the number one keyword and now you want to scale it up, now you want to put more people into your funnel, now you want to drive more people from that number one ranking, what do you do? Well, there's nothing you can do because there's only so many searches that take place per month and out of those so many searches, you're only going to get so many clicks. It's a passive traffic generation method because there's nothing that you can do to scale it up, there's nothing that you can do to use that number one ranking to dump more and more people into your funnel. Now on the flip side with paid media, paid traffic, media buying, you have the ability to scale it up as big as you want, keep it as small as you want, scale it up as fast as you want, slow it down, pump it up, at will, literally at will. The other thing is that when you use things like SEO, right, you are at the mercy of Google, we already know that there's what, Panda, Penguin, Rogues. Mike's Tigers and Bears. Exactly, I don't even know where their algorithm has changed and what was once viewed as a positive thing by Google is now no longer viewed as a positive. But at one point it was all about links, links, links, links, links, now it's not about links, it's about social signals, right? And you know, things have constantly changed with Google and so what happened to a lot of websites, a lot of marketers is they went from top rankings, top three rankings to top 113 rankings where nobody was seeing their site and that could happen overnight. Now imagine if you were a marketer who had a business that was reliant on those rankings, you could go from a business one day to bankruptcy the next or your business to a ghost town the next simply because Google makes an algorithm change because it is a passive activity. And so I will say that is there value in free traffic? Absolutely, there's value in all kinds of targeted traffic. The key is that you're not reliant on that form of traffic, that your business wouldn't disappear, your sales wouldn't evaporate if those channels, those sources of traffic disappeared, right? To me they're like it's like the icing on the cake, it's like the extra dessert on the side. Whatever traffic we get from our search engine optimization and our articles and our videos great but you should be reliant on the thing that you can control which is paid media Now let me just say something very quickly and then we're going to get back to more questions and that is that most people they think about paid media wrong, like they think about, they look at the numbers and they're like man I got to lay out all this money, I got to have a budget of 2000, 1000, 5000, 10,600 whatever and they're like well what does my conversion have to be and blah blah blah, listen let me simplify the whole process for you. At the end of the day the reason why you create a marketing funnel like the six figure funnel formula shows you how to do is so that you can take one dollar and turn it into a dollar or two dollars or three dollars or more of sales from dollar one. You know I had somebody on the webinar last night with I think it was Don Crowther's webinar that I was on asked me, she said well how big does my list need to be to really begin monetizing it. And I said that's the wrong question to ask because it has nothing to do with list size, you begin monetizing from visitor number one, from the very first visitor, the very first dollar that's invested. You should be able to see the return on investment from your marketing funnel when you invest your first 50 bucks into traffic generation period, end of the story. Now you might see that there's something missing like or you know we need to optimize the funnel a little bit more, I'm not saying that you're guaranteed to make profit or make money right from that $50 but you'll see right away, we don't need to wait until you get a thousand people or five thousand people or anything like that. And so for time purposes Charles, so somebody there's a, let me see here, okay, so somebody asked can you talk about maximizing solo ads? Yeah, there's a couple things, now you have to be smart about solo ads, hey the fastest way to make a whole lot more money with solo ads is to use retargeting built into your ads. Now this is what happens, let's say that we buy a solo ad today, we get 2000 clicks to our website today. Most people, you know they get the 2000 clicks, let's just say we got a 50% conversion rate, we got a thousand brand new people on our email list, hallelujah we move on, okay what happened to those thousand other people that have never heard of you before, that just saw your landing page and you go man I don't know about this and then they leave. You need to use retargeting, there's AdRoll, there's Perfect Audience, there's actually a number of them, Facebook even has built in retargeting. And for those of you who aren't familiar with retargeting that means you visit a page and something happens, they set a cookie and then everywhere you go on the internet you see this banner banner banner banner. The big issue with solo mailing is typically you do a solo mailing to a landing page, the people that I've been, they've trusted you, have taken a chance and go okay I will allow you to send me emails. Maximize the people that did not opt in, start using retargeting banners to build a relationship with them, to get them to come back to the page, get them to opt in. That is the easiest way to do it. The second thing is most people use what's called a blind offer, that means you get to the landing page, I'm guilty as this is the next person and it's like headline, opt in box, give me your name, email, I don't know you from Adam's house cat and as marketers we go well my logo is on the page, you should recognize the logo because the logo is like I'm the logo, recognize the logo, prospects, we remember our logo where prospects can't. One of the things that we started doing is we started using the blind squeeze page, we got exponentially higher opt in rates but when we started going back and running the emails through Bright Verify we're like oh something's not right. We get a lot more bogus emails or a lot more emails where it's the disposable email, it's the email address I check like when I need to go get my PayPal receipt, so to maximize your solo mailing, start using retargeting and using landing pages, anytime you can put your own face on the landing page, if I could I'd brand my name on everybody's landing page if I could, but you want to, if you can use your image, your face, you know let people know hey I'm a real person, if you can use video on a solo mailing, your opt in's that you get will be a higher quality opt in because they're like okay I trust you, you're legit, so definitely take advantage of your picture, video if all possible, retargeting that will help you maximize your emails or your solo mailings. Awesome man, awesome, okay so let's cruise through a couple more questions, so somebody asked what tools do you use for A.B. split tests and how do you decide what are the right changes to make? Okay, I'm now, most people split tests, I split tests looking at did it increase my revenue, my EPC earnings per click, you can have a high, you can get really high opt in rates on a squeeze page but they don't necessarily equate to more money, more sales, you know you can opt in, hey guess what I've got, you know, opt in here I'm going to give you you know five PLR products, a cat, a dog and I'm going to throw in you know a lucky charm something like that, the reality of it is when you're split testing you need to be looking at it, is it increasing revenue, that's the driver at the end of the day or and that is if you're trying to make a sale, if you're just trying to generate leads on a list, do it, do it, opt in, hit it and get it but what we use typically if you're using lead page has they have built in split testing so you can come in here and say, let me page A, let me page B, lead pages will actually do the split testing for you, we use a number of different products, typically it depends on the traffic source because, given a good example, I love visual website optimizer, it's a great product, it's simple to use basically WordPress plug-in, you go into their interface and say page A, page B and the people that get to page C, great, it's really very simple, very easy, what it doesn't lend itself to is certain types of traffic, when we're doing what's called CPV or PPV traffic, we may be hammering the server at a rate of maybe five or six thousand clicks an hour and unfortunately, think about this, visual website optimizer, they have different plans and their plan may be like seventy nine dollars for ten thousand page views, I can blow that up in two hours and I don't want to have to spend a lot of money, so we also use things like add tracks gold, very simple, down, dirty, gets the job done, we also use things like CPV labs because it has built in split testing and it depends on the traffic source we're using but if you're using solo mailing, CPV labs is not free, CPV labs works best with PPV traffic, media buys, add tracks gold works great for solo emails and if you're going to be doing something like Facebook you can use visual website optimizer, so it depends on the volume of traffic you're getting and the end purpose of what you're doing, so hopefully that helped. Cool man and I'll add to that with, I will add to that by saying that we use, you know, look for split testing you could absolutely use, you could use Google analytics, the experiments feature on Google analytics and that will do just fine for you and that's free, right, that will allow you to do some simple split testing, simple A B split testing where you will send a certain percentage of the traffic to one page and a certain percentage of traffic to another page and it will monitor which page does better for you depending on what the goal is, if the goal is to generate a lead it will show you which one has a higher opt in rate let's say. The advantage to using a paid tool like something like visual website optimizer is that there's a lot of other features that you can use that allow you to do some more advanced things like you can actually track sales, right, and so you can track how many sales you've made in term and you can track money, dollars, and cents as well there's a cool feature within visual website optimizer that allows you to, allows you to split test different elements within your funnel without having to create multiple pages, so for example you could take your traffic activation page and with visual website optimizer you could split test multiple headlines, multiple hooks on the traffic activation page without having to create a whole bunch of different pages because visual website optimizer, excuse me, has a very cool built in JavaScript like editor where very similar to something like lead pages where you open up the page that you want to test inside a visual website optimizer and you click on the headline let's just say the hook, well it'll let you create a variation right there where you just click on it you say you know you click I want to create a variation you type in the new variation and boom and what visual website optimizer does for you with some JavaScript is without needing a new URL without needing to create a new page it will dynamically show half of the traffic or whatever percentage you set the one variation of the headline and the other half of the traffic it'll show the other variation and so you can do that with multiple things you can also do that with an image if you had an image on a particular page let's just say hypothetically whatever you know an image of you you could test different images without having to create new pages you literally with visual website optimizer just click on the image it'll ask for a new variation you boom you upload your image and then it will automatically show two different two different variations for you the recently like yesterday I think I had five webinars or some insane number of webinars and it was a common theme you know people were constantly asking questions like well what should my opt-in rate be you know like what should my conversion rate be on my on my customer captivation offer and and and what you know what should my conversion rate be on my add-on offers through my transaction maximization sequence and let me let me first say this that what Charles said is so spot-on that I don't want you to miss it and that is that for those of you that are just starting out the most important number the only number that you should really that you should really care about the only number that matters the only number that means anything is return on investment it doesn't matter what your opt-in rate is what your conversion rate is on your sales on your on your customer captivation offer you know or if you're if you're not using the six-figure funnel formula it doesn't matter what what those numbers are if the return on investment isn't there like I had a client come to me and he came into the program and he had a 50% rate a 50% opt-in rate 50% and he was ecstatic right he had a 50% opt-in rate on his on his particular squeeze page that he was using it and and and but he wasn't making sales and so when we ended up when I ended up helping him to create his funnel and and I said you know now it's time that you know look we need to get rid of this squeeze page I mean he said but it's getting a 50% opt-in rate and I said but it's not generating sales and so who cares that it's getting a 50% opt-in rate would you rather get a 50% opt-in rate make no sales or a 10% opt-in rate and and generate sales see like at the end of the day it doesn't matter what the it doesn't matter what the opt-in rate is if the return on investment isn't there the first number that you always look at the first number that you go to the first metric that you go to is the return on investment and there's a variety of different ways to look at that right like earnings per click is certainly one of the metrics that you need to know you know a return on investment is the end number what I mean by that is that you know is that like you know how much did you earn per click there's a couple different ways that you can that some marketers look at that number right so you know earnings per click meaning for how much did I generate in sales for every click that I've gotten well that number is a great number to know but it doesn't mean anything if you don't know how much did it cost you to generate a click or to generate a visitor right knowing what your average visitor value is is only one side of the coin right you need to know how much is the average visitor worth to you because that tells you how much you can afford to pay to get the average visitor to your site like somebody might say well I have a $10 average visitor value well I don't is that good I don't know are you spending 15 bucks to get a visitor to your website if so then no that's not good because you're losing five bucks for every visitor right is $5 per is $5 good or just is $5 a $5 average visitor value or EPC bad well it depends it could be great if it only cost you a dollar to generate a visitor to your website so the return on investment is kind of taking those two critical metrics and just looking at the end number looking at the end result meaning for every dollar that you've invested how much money did you make back that's the first priority that's the first thing you look at when it comes to optimizing your funnel when it comes to improving the results enhancing the results what it becomes about at that point is looking at opt-in read is looking at conversion rate is looking at click-through rate looking at all of the elements in the funnel right to see where the constraint is in the funnel like without going too deep into this I'll just tell you this that that's that when it comes time to optimize your funnel what you look for is you look for the weak link in the chain I want you to look at your funnel as if it is a chain and the chain is made up of links you've got the traffic captivation page that's one link you've got the customer captivation offer that's another link you've got you know add on one two three and and and and four and and and so on you've got right all these different links in the in the chain well if you wanted to strengthen the chain you've got to find the weakest link it does not matter if you if you go to that chain and you find any other link besides the weakest link and you strengthen that link the chain is still going to be only as strong as the weakest link and so what you're looking for in your marketing funnel is you're looking for that weak link and the way we identify that weak link is to look at the different metrics at the different stages and to see which one according to the metrics is showing us that this is the weak link right it's showing us that this is the weak link and so then we go and we optimize it so the value of knowing like click-through rate and opt-in rate and sales conversion rate is is only really valuable when you're optimizing the funnel but early on the only thing that you can deposit is return on investment can't deposit opt-in rate you can't deposit click-through rate you can't deposit you know sales conversion rate the only thing that you can deposit the only number that tells you whether you're making money losing money or breaking even is return on investment and so this according process that you use do is is that a different process that you use specifically with social media folks is there a different funnel that you're using you know that you're driving people from Facebook into or are you driving people regardless of the source like are you driving Facebook people into the same funnel that you're driving you know other traffic into well I don't so here's the way I design my my opt-in systems they're based around subject area not based upon where they came from because if someone is interested in getting six pack abs I don't care whether they came from social media and add a webinar that I've done or someone seeing my business card at a at a conference they all are still interested in six pack abs so I bring them into the six pack abs opt-in sequence and then they go into the six pack abs funnel I don't have a six pack abs funnel I don't even have a six pack app but I did once and so I'm just using that as an example here and so that's the way I do do it and one of the key things that I believe is that most of us make a huge error when it comes to opt-in incentives and that is we assume that one does the job actually you should have five to fifteen for each subject area that you are teaching why because there are different types of people who are interested there are people have different questions and different situations that they're coming from where if you only have one opt-in incentive people are going to say well that doesn't fit me but if you got five to fifteen one of those is going to fit them that's going to bring them in and then that eventually leads into your funnel you're broadening the top of the funnel so you can get more people in okay um okay that's interesting and so we've talked about um facebook and so where does something like twitter um you know pinterest linkedin where are some of these other uh others come into play okay so which so we've already talked about the most important site in your social media arsenal which is your blog what is the site the social media property that gets the highest dollar sales per click say that again which social media site generates the highest dollar sales per click twitter uh no it was true about three years ago but not anymore uh pinterest pinterest it is ding ding ding ding you want it on the second try okay so facebook will always generate more clicks but the quality the purchase quality of the individuals that come from facebook is considerably lower than pinterest now there's a real reason for that that i think is real key for everyone to understand here and that is when you go on let's let's face the facts on facebook you're collecting friends it's where you go to hang out with your friends with your friends exactly learn more about them maybe figure out where you're going on friday night maybe just see the pictures of their kids whatever it is the whatever this marketers to try to sell something using facebook we have to interrupt their conversation with their friends about friendship kinds of things and say hey pay attention to this thing over on the side that's called interruption marketing now there are many ways to do that very effectively and i have nothing against pin facebook in terms of that but pinterest is a totally different mindset and that's why it works so well so facebook literally is all about the conversation that's why it's all structured pinterest on the other side hardly has any conversation going on at all pinterest is a is what i call an aspirational site where people post pictures of things that they aspire to in their lives so it can be i aspire to own this dress or these shoes it could be i aspire to look skinny like this it could be i want to have this car it could be i want to have a spouse who loves me this way all those things are aspirational and so what we are doing when we post on pinterest if we want to succeed we focus on the aspirational aspects of whatever it is that we sell we post pictures that meet those aspirational aspects which then cause other people to then re-pin that spreading your word even further and getting even more and more clicks plus because it is aspirational that means that many of the things that are that they aspire to are things they're willing to pay money to get so when they go and look at their list of pins and they're clicking on them they get there they click on it because they want that in their life so then when they're coming to your site they're coming from a vision of i want this thing not because i got interrupted with my chat with my friends and so they're much more likely to actually put down dollars once they get there and so for pinterest how does the typical marketer like let's just take for argument's sake the typical info marketer or affiliate marketer how do you even tap into pinterest like like if there were five steps like what would those steps be what do you even do with pinterest okay so and the key being at the foundation of that is to you know to use it to truly build your business not yet not just another potential you know avenue to explore i mean that's the way i think i know i you know to me social media okay personally i love seeing my grandkids pictures and those kinds of things but to me social media i focus solely on the building business part okay so let's just tell you what i'll just give you a formula that works really really well two elements in that formula two paths path number one think about your business from a pictorial point of view what are the images that will cause people to be interested in getting more from your business whether those are pictures of the products that you sell yes so let's use a specific example don let's use your six pack abs let's say you're selling an information product on how to get six pack abs so okay so go with that okay so obviously pictures of six pack abs and male and female which i realize is hard to find on females six pack abs because their bodies just aren't designed the same way but great looking abs okay so that's that's one which directly shows here's what you get as a result of what i do but people will get tired of any board that just has 1000 pictures of six pack abs so you want to start looking at some of the other secondary kinds of benefits that come from that which is health which is feeling good which is relationships that come from looking good and feeling good and so you start building out all the levels there's the core level of the direct features that they get and then there's the benefit then there's the end benefit so realize there's a difference between a benefit and end benefit real quick example when i worked for conagra i worked on the banquet brand okay so banquet chicken so that's the the 99 cent tv dinners okay so we're preparing to do an ad and we're out doing some research with people and what we quickly discover is if we show a mom so most banquet dinners are made by mom when she's just going out and she needs to put something in front of the kids they'll eat for the babysitter or she's tired of the kids making complaining about food whatever it is so it's they're rarely eaten by the parents they're mostly eaten by the kids okay so mom you show a picture of mom putting a plate in front of the kid the kids face lights up why because it's got the cherry cobbler in it and the kid immediately lights up and does that okay that's a benefit my kid will eat this my kid will be happy okay that's a benefit an end benefit is why does mom do all this mom does this because she wants her kid to be happy okay but what she really does it for is that she wants to feel loved what we discovered is if we all we did is had that kid turn to mom and say thank you mom our commercials skyrocketed effectiveness but when we added one more piece when the kid reached around mom's leg and gave it a hug and said thank you mom all of us and we just attached not a benefit not an end benefit we attacked an end-end benefit we got to what mom really wants which is she wants to feel loved by her kid okay so what's the benefit of six pack abs what's the end benefit of six pack abs what's the end-end benefit of six pack abs and each one of those goes down different routes so you can go with health you can go with relationships you can go with long life you can go with feeling better you can go with lots of different things you just have to choose which ones you want to target got it okay okay so that's the first avenue in pinterest may i share with you the second or you want to ask questions about the go go keep going the second one there are a lot of people who come to me and they say don my business is ugly i'm a roto router guy we don't want to show them pictures of what it is that happens when i come into their home okay and i totally agree with that okay so in those cases where your business is not particularly pretty what you can then do is that you can create a set of tips that are picture-based that tell them how to achieve the goal that they want to reach so you can put out a series of six pack ab tips and here's tip number 43 six pack ab tip number 43 first off what is and you actually write that on on the on the picture first off what does that tell them tells them there's at least 42 more and there might be even more coming after that so all of a sudden they immediately wow there's lots of stuff happening here i want to pay attention to this then down below you write the tip and you put a url where they can find out more just a few words in that tip so the best way to do this is to write a blog post that gives them this tip explained you take this picture and you put tip number 43 on it and you put that out and then you pin that picture from your blog post so that when they click on your picture they come to your blog post it gives them more information you now have 43 different blog posts all about how to get six pack abs every one of them has a Pinterest pin that is tied to that which by the way you took that same picture and posted it to twitter and to facebook and you've got a systemic traffic driving machine that's already been built once they arrive you then give them an offer to get more information about this that offer can be a low priced offer as you said it can be an opt-in incentive or it can be a high priced offer whatever it is works best for your particular funnel setup cool okay so in the time that we have remaining two things one i want to give you the opportunity to share something about using social media to to drive people into a new funnel that maybe you didn't get the chance to share or that i didn't ask you and then after that i want you to okay all right so let me give one mistake and then i'll answer and then i'll i'll give the final thing the biggest mistake you can make in social media and this is one of the most popular things to do on social media is to have a contest where you give away an ipad why is that because people are coming because they want the ipad not because they want your stuff which means that once they have entered and you're sending them all this stuff they're not paying any attention to it which makes your edge rank score which is the ranking system the facebook uses to determine how often people actually see your stuff it plummets which means no one's seeing your stuff which means that giving away the ipad may have gotten hey 10 000 more people to follow you on social media and three of them are actually interested in what it is you have to sell so don't make that mistake the so the the one piece of advice that i would give to everyone is to sit down and strategically think through the process what does your consumer want what are the major questions that they have what are the major issues that they have turn that into a list and turn that into a series of blog posts and social media posts that support those go ahead make the videos and put them all up yes this sounds like work but guess what if you don't work you're not going to get some you're not going to get the results that come out of it ultimately social media the way it monetizes it is that you send them from social media into which then sends them on to something that monetizes it social media itself rarely directly monetizes very cool awesome i think that's a great way to wrap up our time and so share with these guys and gals um where can they go to learn more from you learn more about social media and discount any of it because there's no savvy in that there's nothing you know let me just say it like this you know don't nobody should pat themselves on the back if they figure out how and reasons why certain things won't work you pat yourself on the back when you take the time to figure out how can i take those things and apply them to my business and my situation and so so you really need to think about that right it's like some of the biggest breakthroughs that occur in business and let me just say this let me pause for a second because i really want you to get that i want that to sink in this whole idea that any schmo can figure out how to discount an idea right any schmo i can ask my neighbor why something won't work and mark my words my neighbor will be able to figure out some reason why it won't work but it's the savvy entrepreneurs the savvy marketers that figure out how it can be used how it can be applied and so that's the way that i want each and every one of you to think about the things that not only you hear from don and all the other experts but even all the things that you come in contact with on a regular basis like the real business innovations the big business innovations come when marketers and companies take ideas from outside their industry see one of the biggest mistakes that that lots of average novice marketers make with their marketing and and i could say this because this was something i did for many many years the first handful of years in fact that i was online is all they do is look at what other internet marketers are doing their whole focus is internally in this little community and they look at what other internet marketers are doing and they then knock off what those other marketers are doing and they don't realize that the savvious marketers are looking outside the internet marketing community they're looking at what's working in other industries for other products for other services and they're figuring out how can we take that and bring that in here right when you see you've seen you know things like cds wall greens pharmacies offering drive-thrus that was from paying attention to other industries to banking right to figuring out what are they doing that's working really well from them and how can we bring it in here again it doesn't take a genius it doesn't take a rocket scientist to figure out why things won't work the real genius the real thing comes from figuring out how can we apply this and make it work and so um so cool so and it really is lisa thank you for that it's kind of cool that i can see 500 buyers within 10 days and it does depend on the market it does depend on a couple other factors but that's a good ballpark general figure because then the cool thing here is this is the hardest part in any marketing is to get somebody to buy something from you once that is the most expensive hardest customer to convert on the planet so once you strike pay dirt you have to stop and say do I go out and build new markets or do I optimize and focus on this particular market and that's typically what we do as soon as we hit those we pull the plug on this and we say okay it's time to take this serious I mean I'm sure you've seen it Todd where people will spend eight or nine months building a whole product line and funnel and everything is perfect and Rosie yet they have not spent one day interacting with any potential customer in the real world and they find out after nine months or a year God forbid three years or longer that their concept does not meet what people want in the real world we do the opposite if I mean if if we can sell with the bare minimum imagine what it could be like now that we're going to do do it properly but that's how we pretty much read the future so to speak on what we should focus on and who we should market to and how we should market by having these little test-fielder products out there first yeah and so just to finish up this section really quick so two things that I know people are you know would find value in learning or understanding more one is is there a particular traffic source that you do migrate towards with these dealer products and what type of budget are you using um or would you recommend a client uses to to do this kind of feeler process yeah absolutely so the first way to do this is um I'm gonna I want to spend less than $500 I want to be able to bootstrap I don't want to throw money to solve a problem my favorite traffic source for me personally is Facebook it's a tricky one though because you have to pick million ways to get traffic it's not even funny so play with those and find out the one that you like the best now here's a little secret it's a different slightly different model but let's just say is as humanly possible and as quick as time as possible so I will stop and during this process usually if I'm already seeing that they're biting on the bait so to speak and I'm getting some play I will start conceptualizing in my head immediately but I like to then go back in my favorite price point to sell it is $500 $499.95 if the market can bear that price and I can deliver on that I know on goal because if you started doing the math 200 customers $500 I mean that's a lot of money that's a six figure business just right there alone right and so that's when we only need 200 customers and so I think that that can happen now some markets they don't spend 500 bucks that's the way at the top top end I want to go to markets where 500 bucks is in the middle now 500 bucks is in the top end I'll probably go down to 100 bucks and say what can I sell for 100 bucks because then if we got a thousand customers who pay us 100 bucks that's a six figure little project for us right there right and so I typically immediately leap to and then I make the product fit the price if we were to sell something at $500 what would it have to look like right and if we were to sell it at $500 how will we have to sell it differently than the product that we sold for 20 or 30 dollars once you take those two things provided with the customers that bought and wrote to you corresponded with you you corresponded with them maybe you were proactive and asking them hey did you get this all right did you understand everything right once you have that's the guts the product once you say this is what a $500 product should look like this is what I'm going to need to do to sell that and this is what my intelligence is based on what these customers are telling me and then you go to work now it's funny because so some people they cannot see themselves ever selling a $500 product Todd but they what they have to understand is in any given market and I learned this from Don Crowther who you just had on here uh Don says to me one day he says Jason you realize that 20% of customers spend 80% of the money in any given market right and I said yeah I should probably just focus mainly on those 20% right now it sucks from a personal standpoint because we all for good people want to help all 100% but I mean we're not in this business just to be friends with everybody right first and foremost we have an obligation to the stockholders to make as much money as possible and usually it's I'm the stockholder of my business you know like I have another partner so I'm 50% but most people let's call you're 100% so you need to optimize profits for you and if that means we have to cut out 80% of the market only focus on the 20 hey that's what it's going to take and so you have to get over your own fears because for most people money is not the primary concern they want answers they want solutions and here's what's really cool guess what I can do to create a $500 product that you can't do if you're only creating a $20 product if nothing else I can give you support beyond reason I could pay $20 in support cost per customer and you'd be bragging even if you're selling 20 bucks and I'm still making $480 in profit right we can build things at a level that's much better so people are actually mad who are the 20% they want to spend the 80% of the dollars because they're not enough people are giving them enough viable options usually to cater to the higher end and that's what we do and that's where all the money is made. Yeah I think and that's a great tip that that really is such an awesome tip from Jason for everybody I think you know it's kind of you know one of the things to think about one of the things to write down and to ponder later on or tomorrow is this idea that the majority of marketers that I come in contact with they underpriced their products significantly and they never find the threshold they never price test to find what it is that their market will bear what it is that their market will happily pay it's more than likely the majority of marketers are leaving a tremendous amount of money on the table from nothing else than just pricing their products too low now look there are lots of times when strategically you want to price your product low like like for example with the six-figure funnel formula training program right that was done strategically it was done strategically to have an impact on the market to blow the market away to create massive buzz and to bring in a ton of excited clients but the reality is unless you're you have a strategic purpose behind doing that one of the first things that I would tell you to do is to test higher and higher prices don't assume you know what the market will bear don't assume you know what that threshold is there is no room for assumptions in direct response marketing direct response marketing is all about the data let the data tell you and so I think that what Jason is saying is is just um it's just spot on it's just is awesome and so um so Jason so um so how do you go about thinking about the product now building it out turning it into a $500 product so you've gotten the result that you wanted from the $19 product you've got the 500 customers in the 10 days and now you're going to take that you're going to turn it into you know uh let's call it a $500 product well what is that process like how do you where do you begin with that yeah there's two easy ways to do it the one is to go very wide and so that's what you say these are the seven or eight or nine things that people are struggling with and maybe each one of those becomes a very hands-on training session and you go you go very wide with it so people get so much value from so many different sources and that's a good way and that's a way that maybe makes sense if you're a super duper really good expert at the subject the other way is you go very very deep with it and that's where you say okay like we can give a really good example here as far as like what do what do internet marketers themselves struggle with well they have trouble a lot of them have trouble like you said registering a domain uh registering a domain is probably a little bit better than the 70% but it's not much better but they have trouble registering a domain they have trouble setting up a wordpress site or a regular html site with on a server and then they have trouble integrating that with their email and then from that also putting up stuff so people can interact on their websites right so how many people in our market alone if you can make a really simple turnkey solution for something like that and maybe it could be training maybe it could be training plus software maybe it could even be a hybrid where you relabel or white label some stuff I don't know right it doesn't make sense doesn't make not make sense but there'd be a lot of people that'd be very happy if for like two or three or four or five clicks of a button something would spit out that would set all that stuff up for them and that's your $500 product right uh you don't really know though so you start looking at do I go super deep and create a solution that is narrow in definition but is smoother and quicker and easier and simpler than what they're used to getting or do I go really really wide with it because a lot of people want to know everything and anything about a topic that they're interested or passionate about but it's always on their mind and both of those have their perks typically if I'm going to do like a nine week training session I will do these live so I will do one session at a time over the next nine weeks I will sell the $500 course and say hey you can join me on these calls so there's certain value in being part of shaping the product itself right and then I'm going to create these bonuses and I'm not on the hook I'm doing all this today right have the stuff that we sell initially in like a new market for $500 doesn't even exist I just say come on and I'll create it with you right and so that's what I typically look like we go very wide or we go very deep and then if you want to get really crazy with thing go wide and deep now we're starting talking about $5,000 products are higher and so okay very cool man so let's say so you're going to do a training program you're going to get paid for it up front and then create it which is always good and so now what do you do with that product are you then taking that product and rolling it back out to the 500 customers that you got are you rolling it out to them and then that becomes your your new front end that you're sending your traffic directly to like how do you now get that offer for that new $500 training out yeah great question first thing we do is we go right back to the same traffic source that generated those new customers second thing we do is go to those new customers third thing we do is we go to other people who have customers like them and say hey how would you like to earn $250 in a sell right let's sit down and talk and figure out a way we can make that happen and so we aggressively start recruiting affiliates now not all niches have affiliates so that's okay then we start buying banner ads instead but that's the what that process looks like and it's amazing how easy it is for you to find people that already have an audience with a compliment of product that you have to that audience and saying hey pop me on on a webinar or send traffic to this video sales letter or what have you and we'll split the profits and there's a never-ending stream of those types of people in a lot of the markets that we play in very cool and so okay so we've got about um malleable what we do is we work backwards and we it's it's very hard to do um but you you're rewarded if you make the sacrifice starting each month we calculate every single expense that's going to be company is my salary uh because personal growth is different than business growth I don't want to just grow my personal bank account which is nice I want to grow both the business and the personal so I'm making more money but my business is also making more money because let me tell you if you go to a uh a bank and you say hey I got nothing this business has been going downhill but yeah my personal account looks great right they'll say yeah we don't want you as a customer right uh so you want to do both ideally right so we say this is how much the business percentage why should grow this month or and that's usually a number from the whole year but I won't get in get crazy with that let's just say this month we need to make net profit uh 150 thousand dollars net profit um and we play with big numbers like that uh it doesn't matter what the number is but you sit down at the beginning of the month and you say okay what are we going to do this month to make that much money well you can start saying this is how much money is going to be coming in automatically based on funnels we already have in place and based on streams of traffic and all that jazz and we know that number because historically it's pretty easy to predict that number once you start tracking it right and then you might have let's say 95 thousand dollars left to cut up the pie with so you can say okay well we have these things that we can relaunch and how much are those going to put a dent into that number and then usually that'll put a significant dent in that number maybe we'll maybe want but then you say so what's the new thing or things I need to launch this month to make the rest of this number right and that's when you start thinking is it going to be a live training will that help me make this number because how many units do I need to sell at this price to the audience that I could reach within this time period right so sometimes depending on what it is it's a product sometimes it's a live training sometimes it's a software uh because we take two things into account how much time and how much money will it make right and certain instances we might find with our back end like we started selling 50 000 service packages uh where I would go in write the webinar presentation for the person line up the jv's for them and all that stuff because we calculated that it would take about one week of my time but net net net to the company's 50 000 because we're not paying affiliates out of that and there's not any hard cost involved with that and so I would have never drained in my life of ever selling a 50 000 package and now we're selling one every other month or every two or three months and that's because we did the numbers and we work backwards now obviously that's only a small part of the business and so then we say if I did the training could I make the number this month probably would be the easiest way to do it right uh because I don't have to create the product up front but then that might cut into next month so we have to be cognizant of that right now here's the sweetest thing possible Todd is when you make the number like halfway into the month then you say these are the other two things I had on the calendar just in case we're going to table those you know and they're going to be like little aces of our sleeves that we can throw out at any given time and then we can take the rest of the month to build the infrastructure because the things you do to get to one million are going to be different than the things you do to get to five million and maybe you can handle the stress in your system right now at one million I guarantee you you're doing the same exact thing you ain't going to handle that five million right and so you have to do some internal stuff either okay and in both of those situations they don't have a clear measurement stick in place on whether they should stay with it whether they should completely abandon it or whether they should change slightly what they're doing and try it again right so I like very tight very small windows where we measure success and failure and then if it's fell we either move on or we try it differently but that's different than saying let me try this and then I get distracted and move on because once we make that decision that we're going to do this and this is our measuring stick we go as all in as about productivity and we um you know Jason shared a little bit of his productivity um uh mindset and tactics and approach if you will I want to share with you one thing I'm also a bit of a uh productivity nut junkie I tend to push myself to get a lot done um and to really crank stuff out um and I want to I want to share with you a productivity tip if you will that is more of a mindset thing more of an action thing that it is that it is anything else and this is something that every single one of you can use and apply and it's just this it really comes from um I really first saw this best explained by um Steven Pressfield it's got a great book out he's got a couple of great books but one of the books that you should read is the war of art not the art of war the old um Sun Tzu book but the war of art by Pressfield Steven Pressfield and in the book um he also has another book which is great I think it's called do the work both are well worth you you grabbing and diving into and one of the things that he says it struck a chord with me the first time I saw it and it's still something that stays in the forefront of my mind and that is that the professional shows up and works right the professional like you know Pressfield Steven Pressfield talks about in his books this idea of resistance and in most cases for most marketers for most entrepreneurs for most people that resistance shows up in the form of procrastination where it is um right it's it's the desire to procrastinate it's the excuses the rationalizations the justifications the it's when you tell yourself I'm not inspired today or I don't have any ideas or I'm drawing a blank or I don't know what to write or I don't know what to say or I I don't know what to do all of that right is what Pressfield would call resistance its resistance you know rearing its ugly head well the idea that that Pressfield presented was that the difference between the pro and the amateurs not the level of resistance that they encounter on a day to day because we all experienced this resistance myself rich jason charles don jonathan um you know you know we all experience resistance but the difference between the pro and the amateur is what you do in response to that resistance right see the amateur is the and this is Pressfield's language now the amateur is the is the person who when is faced with that resistance they um they uh they give in they they they submit to it they let the resistance win when when they're having when they're making rationalizations justifications in their mind when in essence they're really coming up with excuses it's cold out it's raining I'm tired I'm sick my throat hurts all things that I've I could have very easily said today I've got a sore throat I'm thoroughly exhausted operating on um few hours of sleep it's gotta be a hundred plus degrees in here with all this there's lots of reasons and excuses that we can all come up with but how you choose to respond to those is ultimately what separates you from operating as an amateur and a pro see the pro shows up and works regardless of whether you've got inspiration regardless of whether you've got ideas regardless of whether you're on fire regardless of whether you're motivated and regardless of whether you want to you don't wait until you want to write a sales letter right I very rarely want to write a script for anything I very rarely want to do any of those things sometimes I might be excited to you know I might I might have uh uh be anxious to but if I have my choice I'd probably sit on the couch eat pizza and watch Family Guide that's me being honest right and so um so the um let me just see something the uh and so it looks like Jonathan is um I think that is Jonathan is potentially having some problems with his internet he's in Hawaii Hawaii and so maybe we'll we'll we can squeeze Jonathan in later I'm gonna let Andy um kind of cover that um but for right now let me let me let me this is my opportunity now to talk about a bunch of killer stuff so I am gonna just drop it now while there's no guests on here and I think Andy stepped out for a minute now I'm gonna drop it like it's hot so to speak so here's the deal right so just to finish up this section right so you know it's all about showing up and doing the work when you're supposed to do it it's why I've said before to clients that my favorite word my favorite definition is the definition for self-discipline because self-discipline is doing the things that you're supposed to do when you're supposed to do them regardless of how you feel and the most important component of that definition in my opinion is regardless of how you feel it's not just about doing the things that you're supposed to do when you want to do them it's doing the things that you're supposed to do when you are supposed to do them regardless of how you feel regardless of what you're thinking regardless of if you think you're ready or not that's the difference between the pro and the the amateur, right? The amateur waits for inspiration, the amateur waits for motivation, the amateur waits for when they're ready, the amateur waits for the perfect circumstance where Pressfield said the pro just shows up and does it. You sit down and you write, whether you feel like it or not, whether you're inspired or not, whether you're tired or not, if you're supposed to do it, you do it, period, end of the story, right? And that's the difference between – that's my productivity tip for you is, you know, is – and if you had that mindset and that mentality, right? A lot more of you would require a lot less productivity books and hacks and granted, I love it. A huge part of my library on the bottom shelf is all productivity, time management, time control. I probably have 15 books on just that topic alone on the bottom shelf over there. But the reality is that it stems from – it stems first and foremost from a mindset, a mindset of, right, like that the pro shows up and works, period, end of the story. And, you know, you can either – you know, we'll use the expression, you can either feed the beast, meaning, you know, look at procrastination like an addiction because it is an addiction for a lot of people. And there's a chemical response that people have when they procrastinate, right? Like, you will have a chemical response when you have to do something and you – a lot of people, they procrastinate on it. There is a – the high achiever, right, will feel guilt. The low achiever might feel a sense of calming and relief. A monkey off their back. Well, the more you feed that beast, the more you feed that monkey, the stronger that craving and the stronger that chemical response becomes. You've got to stop feeding it. And what happens is, over time, the craving will die, right? Just like a drug. I hate to use that, but just like a drug, that craving, that desire begins to become less and less. And so, recognize that the more you fight that, it's like a muscle, right? The more you resist, the stronger you get at it, the better you get at it. You'll still have resistance. You'll still have things that pop up that you need to deal with that. You'll still have resistance, but you'll be stronger at dealing with it, right? It's like the resistance is a bully that decreases in strength as you increase in strength. The bully's still around, but now you can beat up the bully a little bit. Now you can manhandle the bully, and the bully's not as powerful as he or she once was. And so, that's my productivity tip for you. Now, before I go on to the next thing, so somebody asked, somebody said, go Todd hard hitting and accurate. Cool, thank you Dave. Eric asked, if that's the bottom shelf, what's the top shelf?
